blocks.body: Future Continuous: Questions and Short Answers — Will + subject + be + verb-ing? for actions in progress in the future and polite questions about plans.

{"level":"B1","intro":"In questions with Future Continuous, we move will before the subject: Will + subject + be + verb-ing?","rules":[{"label":"Yes/No question","color":"emerald","text":"Start with Will + subject + be + verb-ing?","example":"Will you be working at 6 p.m.?"},{"label":"Wh-question","color":"blue","text":"The question word comes before will.","example":"What will she be doing later tonight?"},{"label":"Short answers","color":"amber","text":"In short answers, use only will or won't.","example":"Yes, I will. / No, I won't."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic patterns","intro":"Look at the main types of questions and short answers.","items":[{"label":"Yes/No","title":"Will + subject + be + verb-ing?","subtitle":"Will they be waiting for us?"},{"label":"Wh-","title":"What / Where / When + will + subject + be + verb-ing?","subtitle":"Where will you be staying?"},{"label":"Short answer","title":"Yes, subject + will. / No, subject + won't.","subtitle":"Yes, she will. / No, they won't."},{"label":"Polite question","title":"Will you be ...?","subtitle":"It often sounds softer than a direct Will you ...?"}]}

{"title":"2. When these questions sound natural","sections":[{"label":"Action in progress at a future moment","color":"emerald","description":"Ask about something that will be happening at a specific future time.","examples":[{"en":"Will you be sleeping at midnight?","ua":"A question about a process at a future time."},{"en":"What will they be doing at 10 a.m.?","ua":"We ask about future activity."}]},{"label":"Polite questions about plans","color":"blue","description":"Future Continuous often helps you ask more softly about plans or arrangements.","examples":[{"en":"Will you be using the meeting room tomorrow?","ua":"A polite question about an arranged situation."},{"en":"Will she be joining us for lunch?","ua":"A softer way to check an arrangement."}]},{"label":"Short answers","color":"amber","description":"In short answers, do not repeat the whole structure unless you need emphasis.","examples":[{"en":"Will he be driving? — Yes, he will.","ua":"A short answer with will."},{"en":"Will they be staying long? — No, they won't.","ua":"A short answer with won't."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick question patterns","intro":"Here are the most common models.","rows":[{"en":"Will you be working tomorrow evening?","ua":"yes/no question","note":"asking about a future action in progress"},{"en":"What will she be doing this time tomorrow?","ua":"wh-question","note":"asking for a detail about a future situation"},{"en":"Will they be staying with us next week?","ua":"polite question","note":"checking a plan or arrangement"},{"en":"Will he be using the car later? — No, he won't.","ua":"short answer","note":"short answer without repeating the whole form"}],"warning":"In short answers, we usually do not say Yes, I will be unless the sentence continues after it."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Forgetting inversion with will.","wrong":"❌ You will be working at 6?","right":"✅ Will you be working at 6?"},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using V1 instead of verb-ing.","wrong":"❌ Will she be work tomorrow?","right":"✅ Will she be working tomorrow?"},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using the wrong short answer.","wrong":"❌ Yes, I will be.","right":"✅ Yes, I will."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["Questions in Future Continuous = Will + subject + be + verb-ing?","Put the wh-word before will: What will you be doing?","In short answers, will or won't is enough.","Future Continuous often sounds more polite when you ask about plans or arrangements."]}
